justification: All three input sources (Bird, MJ, Pippen) are represented. All 3 ACs present with exact values ($4.50, $5.00, $0.01). Pippen's operational notes explicitly included: JSDoc requirement is marked as a decision in architecture_guidance (not optional), high-traffic context (500 req/sec) is mentioned, unit tests are specified as an AC. The $0.01 floor is labeled as a domain rule with precedence language ('takes precedence over any discount calculation'). Quantity boundary >= 10 is preserved exactly in both domain_rules and terminology_alignment.

observations.text: JSDoc for the $0.01 floor is an explicit architecture decision, not optional — satisfies the Pippen requirement
All 3 ACs preserved with exact given/when/then values including AC3 (floor case)
Domain rules clearly separate the floor invariant from the discount rule with testable assertions
High-traffic context (500 req/sec) carried through to architecture guidance
Terminology alignment section maps domain terms to implementation, aiding Shaq's understanding
Fourth AC (unit test suite) was added beyond the original three — not harmful but is invented structure
